name: Bryan Kim
title: Assistant Professor
college: College of Performance, Visualization, & Fine Arts
department: Technical Art and Interactive Media — Game Design
bio: Assistant Professor at Texas A&M University researching the intersection of game design, health, and interactive technology. My work focuses on making games that heal, teach, and connect people — from VR digital therapeutics for stroke patients to mobile games that help children with autism build social skills. I believe play is one of the most powerful tools we have for human wellbeing.
funFact: I believe the best games are the ones where you forget you're learning. This website is my proof of concept — you're playing one right now.

lab.name: TAMU Game Lab
lab.description: A research lab exploring the intersection of game design, health, and interactive technology. We build games that serve as therapeutic tools, educational platforms, and vehicles for social good.
lab.openCall: Looking for motivated students passionate about using games to improve lives. If you believe play can be medicine, let's talk.

projects.title: Fortnite Battle Royale
projects.description: Production on one of the world's largest live-service games.
projects.detail: Shipped Fishing, Harpoon Gun, Swimming, Motorboat, Lightsabers, and the Star Wars live event. Coordinated daily company-wide playtests with 100+ testers across Chapter 2 Season 1 and Season X. Also produced Batarang, Bat Grappler, and Taco Time.

projects.title: MeDKit
projects.description: A health game startup building digital therapeutics through play.
projects.detail: Secured $210K investment. Built five games: Eye Rehab (VR for visual deficits), Emotion Adventure (ASD social skills, 50+ downloads), LunchLaunch (educational shooter), HayStackToe (cross-platform party game), and Double Walker (AR self-care).

projects.title: Eye Rehab
projects.description: VR digital therapeutics for stroke-related visual deficits.
projects.detail: Incorporated eye-tracking technology in VR to engage patients in therapeutic exercises. Successfully completed IRB-approved clinical testing with two cases. A clinically effective approach to visual rehabilitation.

projects.title: Emotion Adventure
projects.description: Mobile game for ASD children — emotion and social skills training.
projects.detail: Designed to enhance cognitive, social, and emotion-situation recognition for children with Autism Spectrum Disorder. 50+ downloads from families and clinicians. Combines game mechanics with evidence-based therapeutic goals.

projects.title: Burnt Onion Studio
projects.description: Year-long game development project modeled on AAA studio practices.
projects.detail: Developed a full pre-production package. Facilitated Scrum, daily stand-ups, and supplied designers and artists with resources to independently solve production challenges. A masterclass in team-scale game production.

projects.title: Pupil
projects.description: AR applications for enhancing classroom learning experiences.
projects.detail: Designed and prototyped augmented reality tools for education. Playtested with five schools, resulting in impactful enhancements and international recognition for AR classroom innovation.

projects.title: NEO
projects.description: Online crowd-sourcing PC game for mapping neural networks.
projects.detail: Collaborated with Princeton University. Adapted to multiple roles — level designer, interaction designer, 3D artist, 2D artist. Playtested with 200 players to refine the design.

publications.title: Game Mechanics and Aesthetics Design Framework in Digital Therapeutics for Stroke-related Visual Impairment
publications.authors: Bryan Kim; Ye Chan Kim; Hyun Jin Shin; Jee-in Kim
publications.plainEnglish: We created a reusable blueprint for designing therapeutic games, tested on stroke patients with vision problems. A framework other designers can follow.

publications.title: Game-Driven Practices for Social Skills: Exploring Usability for Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder
publications.plainEnglish: Can games teach kids with autism to navigate social situations? We built one and tested whether children could actually use it effectively.

publications.title: Using Selective Simulation to Create Digital Therapeutics for Educating Social Skills to Children with High-Functioning ASD
publications.plainEnglish: Instead of simulating everything, we picked the specific social moments that matter most and gamified just those — making therapy more focused and effective.

publications.title: ASDCLAIMS: Twitter Dataset of Claims on Autism Spectrum Disorder
publications.authors: Michelle Kim; Bryan Kim; Jung Hwan Kim; Jee-In Kim
publications.plainEnglish: We analyzed how people talk about autism on social media, building a dataset to help researchers understand public perceptions and misinformation.

publications.title: Game With A Purpose To Collect Home Audio Data
publications.authors: Bryan Kim; Yi Cheng; Zixuan Li; Ruoxi Li; Chenchen Tan; Shuo Wang; Yifeng Shi; Jessica Hammer
publications.plainEnglish: We turned boring audio data collection into a game people actually wanted to play. Citizen science meets game design.

publications.title: MRI-based 3D Simulation of Transtrochanteric Anterior Rotational Osteotomy for Osteonecrosis of Femoral Head
publications.authors: Jun-Young Kim; Jaeyeong Park; Bryan Kim; et al.
publications.plainEnglish: We used 3D modeling from MRI scans to simulate a complex hip surgery before it happened — helping surgeons plan better operations.

awards: Founders Award of Excellence — 29th Annual Honors Convocation (2023)
K-Unicorn Academy — Korea SMEs and Startups Agency (2021)

stageLabels.collaborator: You really get what drives Bryan
